{{Infobox group
| name = Iron Legion
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Iron Legion Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron]] [[Legion]] is a pervasive and aggressive entity characterized by its fusion of biological matter and mechanical technology. Their fleet consists of ships that are part machine and part flesh, often constructed by stealing existing vessels and infecting them with organic material. This combination results in a collection of gruesome, trophy-like crafts that operate with high levels of mechanical coordination. The [[Legion]] functions like a virus or disease, spreading through systems by consuming both organic and mechanical matter to replicate itself. They utilize underground factories known as [[Scourge Warrens]] to facilitate this expansion, and their presence is marked by massive structures called [[Iron Hives]]. While the [[Legion]] often appears to act as a mindless force driven by an instinct to devour and propagate, they are capable of organized direction under the leadership of an [[Iron King]]. The [[King]] is a masterwork of flesh and [[steel]], standing ten feet tall and possessing abilities comparable to most Karoshans. The primary objective of the [[Legion]] is total assimilation, seeking to incorporate all things into their collective. Their military strategy relies heavily on overwhelming numbers rather than individual ship quality, though they have been known to engage in aggressive incursions against the [[Galactic Union]] and other territories. Despite their reputation as villains, some have expressed interest in repurposing their manufacturing and expansion capabilities for other ends.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron]] [[Legion]] is an enemy military force composed of a swarm of abominations consisting of machines forcibly fused with living beings. Operating from [[Hives]], this group utilizes various technologies including hunter-ships, drop-pods, and short-range boarding devices known as [[Penetrators]] to conduct aggressive attacks. While they have functioned without a leader by mindlessly devouring and replicating following the destruction of their [[King]], they also utilize Bishops for control and possess a drive to make everything part of the [[Legion]]. Their massive fleets, which include thousand-yard battleships made of half-metal and half-flesh, can enter systems from [[Subspace]] to invade territories and colonies. Currently, the [[Legion]] has caused significant damage to [[The Last Horizon]] and continues to push aggressively into [[Galactic Union]] territory.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a swarm of abominations consisting of machines forcibly fused with living beings.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3">''The Captain'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] had a ritual room in the [[Hive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A ritual was intended to raise a new [[King of the Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] found the narrator.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c6">''The Captain'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] operates ships.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c7">''The Captain'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] can enter the system from [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] contains [[Pawns]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] uses hunter-ships.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c9">''The Captain'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] sent a large ship out of [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]]'s ship is a battleship approximately a thousand yards long.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] uses [[Penetrators]] as short-range boarding devices.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10">''The Captain'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is currently leaderless due to the destruction of their [[King]] a year ago.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] produces specific types of technology.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c11">''The Captain'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is approaching the [[Captain]]'s sleeping body.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c13">''The Captain'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is attacking.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is an enemy force.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] possesses battleships.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] creates battleships that are half-metal and half-flesh.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] creates abominations known as [[Reavers]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c17">''The Captain'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Fallen Sword]] has a personal crusade against the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18">''The Captain'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] does not play by fair rules.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] possesses [[Hives]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19">''The Captain'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that [[Varic Vallenar]] intends to wipe out.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola]] mentions the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the crew aims to destroy.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] belongs in [[Dark Space]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that can be led by a [[King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that [[Sola]] works to dismantle.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c26">''The Captain'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is controlled by Bishops.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] acts without a leader by mindlessly devouring and replicating to propagate their existence.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] received the [[Mirror of Silence]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group associated with the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29">''The Captain'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] has ships surrounding [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is capable of moving a huge fleet.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]]'s universal drive is to make everything part of the [[Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Iron]] [[Legion creatures]] can be found inside colonies.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Behemoth]] is a trump card of the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32">''The Captain'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] moves with a unity suggesting the guidance of a [[King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33">''The Captain'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is an endless force with an unlimited commander.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34">''The Captain'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] has fleets that can be dismantled or annihilated.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] has caused significant damage to [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the characters wish to avoid.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] has a [[King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a military force capable of attacking almost anywhere with [[Hives]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36">''The Captain'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] has been pushing into [[Galactic Union]] territory aggressively.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c37">''The Captain'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that destroys copies of [[Vallenar]]'s father and invades the [[Galactic Union]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the protagonist has seen conquer enough planets to be impossible to dislodge.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39">''The Captain'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is the target of invading viruses seeking its heart.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that may take over [[Jak]]'s planet.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40">''The Captain'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is an entity that [[Omega]] mentions in his recitation of the ultimatum.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that uses [[Hives]] and [[Hunter-ships]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] uses drop-pods.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the characters are engaged in a crusade against.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] maintains a link to the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Captain]] requested the destruction of the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c48">''The Captain'', chapter 48</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Following the recent destruction of their [[Grand Hive]], the [[Iron]] [[Legion]] emerged as a priority concern for the [[High Council of the Advocates]]. This group, which had previously been fought by [[Starhammer]] during his conquest, utilizes Bishops capable of deploying various spells, viruses, and parasites, while also turning [[Contrasaurs]] into Behemoths. During their campaign, the [[Legion]] cut through [[Jak]]'s system fleet and deployed a hundred thousand [[Pawns]] from a [[Hive]] that resembles a moon at night. Despite their ability to outnumber the narrator's forces and their full strength facing down [[The Last Horizon]], the [[Legion]] eventually fell after being targeted by [[Sola]] and fought by [[Varic]]'s crew.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]]'s [[Grand Hive]] was recently destroyed.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c3">''The Engineer'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] was one of the groups [[Starhammer]] fought during his conquest.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c7">''The Engineer'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] is a priority concern for the [[High Council of the Advocates]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] has made [[Contrasaurs]] into a [[Behemoth]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the narrator requested to be destroyed.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18">''The Engineer'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] uses Bishops who can deploy spells, viruses, mental pulses, high-frequency vibrations, acid mists, or parasites.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c21">''The Engineer'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]] was brought on to handle the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] cut through [[Jak]]'s system fleet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22">''The Engineer'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] has a [[Hive]] that looks like a moon at night.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] can deploy a hundred thousand [[Pawns]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that could outnumber the narrator's forces.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] has fallen.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that [[Varic]]'s crew fought against.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]]'s full force faced down [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Iron Legion]] is mentioned in comparison to the magical defenses being used.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Spanning across the galaxy, the [[Iron]] [[Legion]] is a group of straightforward villains that spreads like a virus without regard for witnesses. Engaged in conflict with the Karoshans, they were responsible for an ambush that resulted in [[Kyri Prest]] losing her arm. While the narrator and [[Raion]]'s crew both fought against them, the [[Legion]] was eventually cleared from the [[Lesser Karoshan system]] and became the subject of a hunt. Though they are known for creating new weapons, their power is such that [[Neth’terith]]'s swarm could become a threat comparable to them, and they were even summoned by [[Starhammer]] to create an [[Iron King]]. To combat them, the [[D’Niss]] employ different tactics.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group responsible for an ambush that cost [[Kyri Prest]] her arm.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c1">''The Knight'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] is in conflict with the Karoshans.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that [[Neth’terith]]'s swarm could become a threat comparable to.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c2">''The Knight'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator fought against the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c5">''The Knight'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] was cleared from the [[Lesser Karoshan system]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|There was a hunt on for the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c10">''The Knight'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] spreads like a virus and does not care about witnesses.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c14">''The Knight'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] is described as having straightforward villains.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c15">''The Knight'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] was summoned by [[Starhammer]] to create an [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] spans across the galaxy.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]]'s crew fought the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c26">''The Knight'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] is a group that the [[D’Niss]] use different tactics against.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Iron Legion]] makes new weapons.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron]] [[Legion]] is an entity that creates objects from twisted [[steel]] and iron, potentially utilizing the conquest of planets to further its service. This group has been engaged in ongoing conflicts with the [[Galactic Union]] for centuries, and their technology has been discovered within biological remains and shipments aboard the [[Vallenar]]. The [[Legion]] utilizes experimental subjects and has left behind victims across various worlds, including the landmass of [[Fathom]]. Because of their dangerous nature and history of taking individuals like the [[Captain]]'s father, figures such as [[Sola]] have conducted mercenary missions and utilized contacts to track their movements. While the [[Legion]] remains ready to serve, groups like the [[Fallen Sword]] are dedicated to their destruction.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Speculation suggests [[The Last Horizon]] may be conquering planets in service to the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2">''The Pilot'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Zenith Processor]] does not approve of the [[Iron Legion]]'s goals or methods.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Captain]]'s father was taken by the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4">''The Pilot'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The piece of flesh found in the station uses [[Iron Legion]] technology.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c11">''The Pilot'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Vallenar]] ship carried a shipment of [[Iron Legion]] pieces.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c12">''The Pilot'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] has experimental subjects.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] is an entity that the characters have potentially dangerous insights on.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Union]] has been fighting the [[Iron Legion]] for centuries.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c22">''The Pilot'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The landmass of the planet [[Fathom]] resembles a victim of the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Solstice]] may intend to create an [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Fallen Sword]] is dedicated to killing the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Galactic Union]] ships in the system resemble the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c28">''The Pilot'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]] has performed mercenary missions against the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]] has contacts who help track [[Iron Legion]] movements.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c35">''The Pilot'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] is ready to serve.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38">''The Pilot'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] is engaged in ongoing conflicts with the [[Galactic Union]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] creates things made of twisted iron and [[steel]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Several planets along the [[Iron Legion]] report withdrawals.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40">''The Pilot'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]] wants to stop the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42">''The Pilot'', chapter 42</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“Now let’s get the Iron Legion off us,” I said.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“We have an Iron Legion battleship over our heads. Now’s the time.”<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“We intend to wipe out the Iron Legion,” I said.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“Contact me if you have trouble with the Iron Legion.”<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“The Legion can attack almost anywhere, if they send a single Hive on a suicide mission.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon groups]]
